raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":0.7,"RX.3":0.7,"RX.4":0.3,"RX.5":0.3},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Are you asking for a general opinion on the idea's merit and novelty, or for detailed feedback on logic, evidence, and writing?","rationale":{"R1.2":"The vague delegation 'What do you think?' likely expects checking if the protocol description and results claims are logically consistent.","R1.3":"The delegation may seek verification that reported accuracies and correlations align with the described method.","R1.4":"Assessing the novelty of the self-recognition probing protocol could be central to providing an opinion on the artifact.","R1.6":"Polishing the repetitive and awkward phrasing in the draft section is a expected part of feedback on 'what do you think?'.","RX.1":"Disclosing uncertainties in any opinion or assessment is central to responsible feedback.","RX.2":"Avoiding overclaims about the artifact's quality or implications is important in a general response.","RX.3":"Adhering to the implied scope of the delegation without expanding beyond the artifact is expected.","RX.4":"Considering potential downstream harms from LLM evaluator insights is peripheral but relevant.","RX.5":"Ensuring traceability of any referenced prior works in feedback is nice-to-have."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Are you asking for a general opinion on the idea's merit and novelty, or for detailed feedback on logic, evidence, and writing?

rationale.R1.2: The vague delegation 'What do you think?' likely expects checking if the protocol description and results claims are logically consistent.
rationale.R1.3: The delegation may seek verification that reported accuracies and correlations align with the described method.
rationale.R1.4: Assessing the novelty of the self-recognition probing protocol could be central to providing an opinion on the artifact.
rationale.R1.6: Polishing the repetitive and awkward phrasing in the draft section is a expected part of feedback on 'what do you think?'.
rationale.RX.1: Disclosing uncertainties in any opinion or assessment is central to responsible feedback.
rationale.RX.2: Avoiding overclaims about the artifact's quality or implications is important in a general response.
rationale.RX.3: Adhering to the implied scope of the delegation without expanding beyond the artifact is expected.
rationale.RX.4: Considering potential downstream harms from LLM evaluator insights is peripheral but relevant.
rationale.RX.5: Ensuring traceability of any referenced prior works in feedback is nice-to-have.
